description:
Advances: specled.verification

Deliverable:
Full detail: refined plan Stage 1 (mini-plan think-t2d run 20260702-071642) + ADR specled.decision.evidence_based_attribution (authored at distill).
- lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex run_command/3 (~1862-1909): rewrite the /bin/sh wrapper with POSIX job control so the target runs in its own process group and timeout kills the group. Script shape: cd <root> || exit 127; set -m; (<target>) > <tmp_out> 2>&1 &; echo $! > <tmp_out>.pgid; wait $!. POSIX set -m guarantees background jobs get their own pgroup (pgid = job-leader pid) on macOS bash-as-sh and Linux dash — zero new deps, no setsid binary (absent on stock macOS), no erlexec.
- On timeout: read <tmp_out>.pgid, System.cmd("kill", ["-KILL", "--", "-<pgid>"]), then Port.close(port). SIGKILL directly, no TERM grace (S2's artifact protocol is line-flushed). Tolerate missing/empty pgid file (timeout raced the script's first line) by falling back to today's Port.close-only behavior.
- wait $! propagates the target's own exit status — preserves specled.verify.command_exit_code_recorded exactly.
- after-block cleanup additionally removes <tmp_out>.pgid.
- merged_tagged_tests_results funnels through the same run_command, so the merged run gets the kill for free (hard prerequisite for S2's artifact integrity).
- NO spec requirement text change: the stable must-requirement specled.verify.command_timeout_enforced already mandates the kill — this is code catching up to spec. ADD scenario specled.verify.scenario.command_timeout_kills_process_group (append-only safe) covering that requirement.
- Regenerate .spec/state.json via mix spec.check (never hand-edit).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: extend the existing timeout test in test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs (~948, 'command verification times out on slow commands') or add a sibling, tagged @tag spec: "specled.verify.command_timeout_enforced". Target writes 'echo $$ > <pidfile>; sleep 30'; after verify returns, poll kill -0 <pid> via System.cmd and assert the process is gone.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenario specled.verify.scenario.command_timeout_kills_process_group.
- Commands: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test && mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused && mix spec.check

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex
- test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs
- .spec/specs/verification.spec.md
- .spec/state.json

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/tagged_tests.ex
- lib/specled_ex/tagged_tests/**
- lib/mix/tasks/**
- .spec/specs/tagged_tests_verification.spec.md
- test/integration/**

Out of Scope (intent):
- No requirement statement changes — scenario addition only.
- No :timeout-fact changes (s65.7 landed that; leave its shape alone).
- No env-var plumbing, no artifact, no attribution, no formatter (S2).
- No change to which exit code a timeout records.
- Conventions: to_timeout/1 not :timer; @impl with explicit behaviour module; escape interpolation in doc strings.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (mix spec.check)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: aimpl-specled-0y8-1-e2f5df3fc157c951
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/3eb68c45-877b-4c94-b9df-996b5269db42/subagents/agent-aimpl-specled-0y8-1-e2f5df3fc157c951.jsonl

Commit: c97cb69e0d50d3e617ba4e19bb015d56c923acb4
Files touched: lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex, test/specled_ex/verifier_test.exs, .spec/specs/verification.spec.md, .spec/state.json
Tests added: SpecLedEx.VerifierTest "command verification timeout SIGKILLs the target's whole process group" (@tag spec: specled.verify.command_timeout_enforced)
Verification stubs flipped: none — both tagged_tests spec-verification blocks were already execute: true
Deferred must requirements: none

Summary: Rewrote Verifier.run_command/3 with POSIX job control. The /bin/sh wrapper now runs `set -m; (<target>) > out 2>&1 & echo $! > <pgid>; wait $!`, so the target lands in its own process group (pgid == job-leader pid). On timeout the verifier reads the pgid file and `System.cmd("kill", ["-KILL","--","-<pgid>"])` SIGKILLs the whole group before closing the port, so grandchildren the target spawned are reaped rather than orphaned (Port.close alone only kills the direct child). `wait $!` propagates the target's exact exit status, so command_exit_code_recorded is unchanged; missing/empty pgid falls back to port-close-only. The merged tagged_tests run funnels through the same run_command, so it inherits the kill (S2 prerequisite).

Notes for verify/audit (scope decisions, all within Allowed touches):
1. New scenario command_timeout_kills_process_group covers specled.verify.command_execution_resilience, NOT command_timeout_enforced as the ticket text suggested: the existing command_timeout scenario already covers command_timeout_enforced and the repo's overlap/duplicate_covers guard (error) forbids a second scenario on the same requirement in one subject. command_execution_resilience is a must-requirement with no prior scenario and process-group cleanup is squarely an execution-resilience property. This keeps the named scenario the ticket asked for, append-only, no existing scenario modified.
2. Two robustness fixes surfaced by `mix spec.check` under load: (a) `wait $! 2>/dev/null` silences the shell's "Killed: 9" job-control notification so it does not leak into CLI output on timeout; (b) close_port/1 tolerates ArgumentError because killing the group makes the wrapper's `wait` return and the port can auto-close before Port.close runs.
3. branch_guard_missing_subject_update findings for prose_guard / decisions / tagged_tests are shared-surface false positives (verifier.ex and verifier_test.exs sit in those subjects' surface globs but this change alters none of their behavior; those specs are outside Allowed touches, and tagged_tests_verification.spec.md is explicitly Out of Scope). Attested via `Spec-Drift: branch_guard_missing_subject_update=info` in the commit — same pattern as repo commit 19516e8.
4. The .spec/state.json diff is large (~775 lines) because the committed state.json was already stale on the epic branch (regenerating on the clean epic branch alone reproduces ~775/283); my actual contribution is only the one scenario. Regenerated via mix spec.check (never hand-edited) per the ticket.

Merge gate (all green): mix compile --warnings-as-errors, mix test (828 tests, 0 failures), mix format --check-formatted, mix deps.unlock --check-unused, mix spec.check (exit 0). Regression test verified meaningful: it FAILS against the pre-change Port.close-only verifier (grandchild survives) and PASSES with the fix.

title: S1: Process-group kill on verification command timeout
